NKJV.1: There was a certain man in Caesarea called Cornelius, a centurion of what was called the Italian Regiment,
NKJV.2: a devout man and one who feared God with all his household, who gave alms generously to the people, and prayed to God always.
NKJV.3: About the ninth hour of the day he saw clearly in a vision an angel of God coming in and saying to him, “Cornelius!”
NKJV.4: And when he observed him, he was afraid, and said, “What is it, lord?” So he said to him, “Your prayers and your alms have come up for a memorial before God.
NKJV.5: Now send men to Joppa, and send for Simon whose surname is Peter.
NKJV.6: He is lodging with Simon, a tanner, whose house is by the sea. He will tell you what you must do.”
NKJV.7: And when the angel who spoke to him had departed, Cornelius called two of his household servants and a devout soldier from among those who waited on him continually.
NKJV.8: So when he had explained all these things to them, he sent them to Joppa.
NKJV.9: The next day, as they went on their journey and drew near the city, Peter went up on the housetop to pray, about the sixth hour.
NKJV.10: Then he became very hungry and wanted to eat; but while they made ready, he fell into a trance
NKJV.11: and saw heaven opened and an object like a great sheet bound at the four corners, descending to him and let down to the earth.
NKJV.12: In it were all kinds of four-footed animals of the earth, wild beasts, creeping things, and birds of the air.
NKJV.13: And a voice came to him, “Rise, Peter; kill and eat.”
NKJV.14: But Peter said, “Not so, Lord! For I have never eaten anything common or unclean.”
NKJV.15: And a voice spoke to him again the second time, “What God has cleansed you must not call common.”
NKJV.16: This was done three times. And the object was taken up into heaven again.
NKJV.17: Now while Peter wondered within himself what this vision which he had seen meant, behold, the men who had been sent from Cornelius had made inquiry for Simon’s house, and stood before the gate.
NKJV.18: And they called and asked whether Simon, whose surname was Peter, was lodging there.
NKJV.19: While Peter thought about the vision, the Spirit said to him, “Behold, three men are seeking you.
NKJV.20: Arise therefore, go down and go with them, doubting nothing; for I have sent them.”
NKJV.21: Then Peter went down to the men who had been sent to him from Cornelius, and said, “Yes, I am he whom you seek. For what reason have you come?”
NKJV.22: And they said, “Cornelius the centurion, a just man, one who fears God and has a good reputation among all the nation of the Jews, was divinely instructed by a holy angel to summon you to his house, and to hear words from you.”
NKJV.23: Then he invited them in and lodged them. On the next day Peter went away with them, and some brethren from Joppa accompanied him.
NKJV.24: And the following day they entered Caesarea. Now Cornelius was waiting for them, and had called together his relatives and close friends.
NKJV.25: As Peter was coming in, Cornelius met him and fell down at his feet and worshiped him.
NKJV.26: But Peter lifted him up, saying, “Stand up; I myself am also a man.”
NKJV.27: And as he talked with him, he went in and found many who had come together.
NKJV.28: Then he said to them, “You know how unlawful it is for a Jewish man to keep company with or go to one of another nation. But God has shown me that I should not call any man common or unclean.
NKJV.29: Therefore I came without objection as soon as I was sent for. I ask, then, for what reason have you sent for me?”
NKJV.30: So Cornelius said, “Four days ago I was fasting until this hour; and at the ninth hour I prayed in my house, and behold, a man stood before me in bright clothing,
NKJV.31: and said, ‘Cornelius, your prayer has been heard, and your alms are remembered in the sight of God.
NKJV.32: Send therefore to Joppa and call Simon here, whose surname is Peter. He is lodging in the house of Simon, a tanner, by the sea. When he comes, he will speak to you.’
NKJV.33: So I sent to you immediately, and you have done well to come. Now therefore, we are all present before God, to hear all the things commanded you by God.”
NKJV.34: Then Peter opened his mouth and said: “In truth I perceive that God shows no partiality.
NKJV.35: But in every nation whoever fears Him and works righteousness is accepted by Him.
NKJV.36: The word which God sent to the children of Israel, preaching peace through Jesus Christ— He is Lord of all—
NKJV.37: that word you know, which was proclaimed throughout all Judea, and began from Galilee after the baptism which John preached:
NKJV.38: how God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power, who went about doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, for God was with Him.
NKJV.39: And we are witnesses of all things which He did both in the land of the Jews and in Jerusalem, whom they killed by hanging on a tree.
NKJV.40: Him God raised up on the third day, and showed Him openly,
NKJV.41: not to all the people, but to witnesses chosen before by God, even to us who ate and drank with Him after He arose from the dead.
NKJV.42: And He commanded us to preach to the people, and to testify that it is He who was ordained by God to be Judge of the living and the dead.
NKJV.43: To Him all the prophets witness that, through His name, whoever believes in Him will receive remission of sins.”
NKJV.44: While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell upon all those who heard the word.
NKJV.45: And those of the circumcision who believed were astonished, as many as came with Peter, because the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out on the Gentiles also.
NKJV.46: For they heard them speak with tongues and magnify God. Then Peter answered,
NKJV.47: “Can anyone forbid water, that these should not be baptized who have received the Holy Spirit just as we have?”
NKJV.48: And he commanded them to be baptized in the name of the Lord. Then they asked him to stay a few days.

ESV.1: At Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, a centurion of what was known as the Italian Cohort,
ESV.2: a devout man who feared God with all his household, gave alms generously to the people, and prayed continually to God.
ESV.3: About the ninth hour of the day  he saw clearly in a vision an angel of God come in and say to him, “Cornelius.”
ESV.4: And he stared at him in terror and said, “What is it, Lord?” And he said to him, “Your prayers and your alms have ascended as a memorial before God.
ESV.5: And now send men to Joppa and bring one Simon who is called Peter.
ESV.6: He is lodging with one Simon, a tanner, whose house is by the sea.”
ESV.7: When the angel who spoke to him had departed, he called two of his servants and a devout soldier from among those who attended him,
ESV.8: and having related everything to them, he sent them to Joppa.
ESV.9: The next day, as they were on their journey and approaching the city, Peter went up on the housetop about the sixth hour  to pray.
ESV.10: And he became hungry and wanted something to eat, but while they were preparing it, he fell into a trance
ESV.11: and saw the heavens opened and something like a great sheet descending, being let down by its four corners upon the earth.
ESV.12: In it were all kinds of animals and reptiles and birds of the air.
ESV.13: And there came a voice to him: “Rise, Peter; kill and eat.”
ESV.14: But Peter said, “By no means, Lord; for I have never eaten anything that is common or unclean.”
ESV.15: And the voice came to him again a second time,  “What God has made clean, do not call common.”
ESV.16: This happened three times, and the thing was taken up at once to heaven.
ESV.17: Now while Peter was inwardly perplexed as to what the vision that he had seen might mean, behold, the men who were sent by Cornelius, having made inquiry for Simon's house, stood at the gate
ESV.18: and called out to ask whether Simon who was called Peter was lodging there.
ESV.19: And while Peter was pondering the vision, the Spirit said to him, “Behold, three men are looking for you.
ESV.20: Rise and go down and accompany them without hesitation,  for I have sent them.”
ESV.21: And Peter went down to the men and said, “I am the one you are looking for. What is the reason for your coming?”
ESV.22: And they said, “Cornelius, a centurion, an upright and God-fearing man, who is well spoken of by the whole Jewish nation, was directed by a holy angel to send for you to come to his house and to hear what you have to say.”
ESV.23: So he invited them in to be his guests. The next day he rose and went away with them, and some of the brothers from Joppa accompanied him.
ESV.24: And on the following day they entered Caesarea. Cornelius was expecting them and had called together his relatives and close friends.
ESV.25: When Peter entered, Cornelius met him and fell down at his feet and worshiped him.
ESV.26: But Peter lifted him up, saying, “Stand up; I too am a man.”
ESV.27: And as he talked with him, he went in and found many persons gathered.
ESV.28: And he said to them, “You yourselves know how unlawful it is for a Jew to associate with or to visit anyone of another nation, but God has shown me that I should not call any person common or unclean.
ESV.29: So when I was sent for, I came without objection. I ask then why you sent for me.”
ESV.30: And Cornelius said, “Four days ago, about this hour, I was praying in my house at the ninth hour,  and behold, a man stood before me in bright clothing
ESV.31: and said, ‘Cornelius, your prayer has been heard and your alms have been remembered before God.
ESV.32: Send therefore to Joppa and ask for Simon who is called Peter. He is lodging in the house of Simon, a tanner, by the sea.’
ESV.33: So I sent for you at once, and you have been kind enough to come. Now therefore we are all here in the presence of God to hear all that you have been commanded by the Lord.”
ESV.34: So Peter opened his mouth and said: “Truly I understand that God shows no partiality,
ESV.35: but in every nation anyone who fears him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
ESV.36: As for the word that he sent to Israel, preaching good news of peace through Jesus Christ (he is Lord of all),
ESV.37: you yourselves know what happened throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee after the baptism that John proclaimed:
ESV.38: how God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power. He went about doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, for God was with him.
ESV.39: And we are witnesses of all that he did both in the country of the Jews and in Jerusalem. They put him to death by hanging him on a tree,
ESV.40: but God raised him on the third day and made him to appear,
ESV.41: not to all the people but to us who had been chosen by God as witnesses, who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.
ESV.42: And he commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one appointed by God to be judge of the living and the dead.
ESV.43: To him all the prophets bear witness that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.”
ESV.44: While Peter was still saying these things, the Holy Spirit fell on all who heard the word.
ESV.45: And the believers from among the circumcised who had come with Peter were amazed, because the gift of the Holy Spirit was poured out even on the Gentiles.
ESV.46: For they were hearing them speaking in tongues and extolling God. Then Peter declared,
ESV.47: “Can anyone withhold water for baptizing these people, who have received the Holy Spirit just as we have?”
ESV.48: And he commanded them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked him to remain for some days.

NASB.1: Now [there was] a man at Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion of what was called the Italian cohort,
NASB.2: a devout man and one who feared God with all his household, and gave many alms to the [Jewish] people and prayed to God continually.
NASB.3: About the ninth hour of the day he clearly saw in a vision an angel of God who had [just] come in and said to him, 'Cornelius!'
NASB.4: And fixing his gaze on him and being much alarmed, he said, 'What is it, Lord?' And he said to him, 'Your prayers and alms have ascended as a memorial before God.
NASB.5: 'Now dispatch [some] men to Joppa and send for a man [named] Simon, who is also called Peter;
NASB.6: he is staying with a tanner [named] Simon, whose house is by the sea.'
NASB.7: When the angel who was speaking to him had left, he summoned two of his servants and a devout soldier of those who were his personal attendants,
NASB.8: and after he had explained everything to them, he sent them to Joppa.
NASB.9: On the next day, as they were on their way and approaching the city, Peter went up on the housetop about the sixth hour to pray.
NASB.10: But he became hungry and was desiring to eat; but while they were making preparations, he fell into a trance;
NASB.11: and he saw the sky opened up, and an object like a great sheet coming down, lowered by four corners to the ground,
NASB.12: and there were in it all [kinds of] four-footed animals and crawling creatures of the earth and birds of the air.
NASB.13: A voice came to him, 'Get up, Peter, kill and eat!'
NASB.14: But Peter said, 'By no means, Lord, for I have never eaten anything unholy and unclean.'
NASB.15: Again a voice [came] to him a second time, 'What God has cleansed, no [longer] consider unholy.'
NASB.16: This happened three times, and immediately the object was taken up into the sky.
NASB.17: Now while Peter was greatly perplexed in mind as to what the vision which he had seen might be, behold, the men who had been sent by Cornelius, having asked directions for Simon's house, appeared at the gate;
NASB.18: and calling out, they were asking whether Simon, who was also called Peter, was staying there.
NASB.19: While Peter was reflecting on the vision, the Spirit said to him, 'Behold, three men are looking for you.
NASB.20: 'But get up, go downstairs and accompany them without misgivings, for I have sent them Myself.'
NASB.21: Peter went down to the men and said, 'Behold, I am the one you are looking for; what is the reason for which you have come?'
NASB.22: They said, 'Cornelius, a centurion, a righteous and God-fearing man well spoken of by the entire nation of the Jews, was [divinely] directed by a holy angel to send for you [to come] to his house and hear a message from you.'
NASB.23: So he invited them in and gave them lodging. And on the next day he got up and went away with them, and some of the brethren from Joppa accompanied him.
NASB.24: On the following day he entered Caesarea. Now Cornelius was waiting for them and had called together his relatives and close friends.
NASB.25: When Peter entered, Cornelius met him, and fell at his feet and worshiped [him].
NASB.26: But Peter raised him up, saying, 'Stand up; I too am [just] a man.'
NASB.27: As he talked with him, he entered and found many people assembled.
NASB.28: And he said to them, 'You yourselves know how unlawful it is for a man who is a Jew to associate with a foreigner or to visit him; and [yet] God has shown me that I should not call any man unholy or unclean.
NASB.29: 'That is why I came without even raising any objection when I was sent for. So I ask for what reason you have sent for me.'
NASB.30: Cornelius said, 'Four days ago to this hour, I was praying in my house during the ninth hour; and behold, a man stood before me in shining garments,
NASB.31: and he said, 'Cornelius, your prayer has been heard and your alms have been remembered before God.
NASB.32: 'Therefore send to Joppa and invite Simon, who is also called Peter, to come to you; he is staying at the house of Simon [the] tanner by the sea.'
NASB.33: 'So I sent for you immediately, and you have been kind enough to come. Now then, we are all here present before God to hear all that you have been commanded by the Lord.'
NASB.34: Opening his mouth, Peter said: 'I most certainly understand [now] that God is not one to show partiality,
NASB.35: but in every nation the man who fears Him and does what is right is welcome to Him.
NASB.36: 'The word which He sent to the sons of Israel, preaching peace through Jesus Christ (He is Lord of all)--
NASB.37: you yourselves know the thing which took place throughout all Judea, starting from Galilee, after the baptism which John proclaimed.
NASB.38: '[You know of] Jesus of Nazareth, how God anointed Him with the Holy Spirit and with power, and [how] He went about doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, for God was with Him.
NASB.39: 'We are witnesses of all the things He did both in the land of the Jews and in Jerusalem. They also put Him to death by hanging Him on a cross.
NASB.40: 'God raised Him up on the third day and granted that He become visible,
NASB.41: not to all the people, but to witnesses who were chosen beforehand by God, [that is], to us who ate and drank with Him after He arose from the dead.
NASB.42: 'And He ordered us to preach to the people, and solemnly to testify that this is the One who has been appointed by God as Judge of the living and the dead.
NASB.43: 'Of Him all the prophets bear witness that through His name everyone who believes in Him receives forgiveness of sins.'
NASB.44: While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell upon all those who were listening to the message.
NASB.45: All the circumcised believers who came with Peter were amazed, because the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out on the Gentiles also.
NASB.46: For they were hearing them speaking with tongues and exalting God. Then Peter answered,
NASB.47: 'Surely no one can refuse the water for these to be baptized who have received the Holy Spirit just as we [did], can he?'
NASB.48: And he ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked him to stay on for a few days.

NLT.1: In Caesarea there lived a Roman army officer named Cornelius, who was a captain of the Italian Regiment.
NLT.2: He was a devout, God-fearing man, as was everyone in his household. He gave generously to the poor and prayed regularly to God.
NLT.3: One afternoon about three o’clock, he had a vision in which he saw an angel of God coming toward him. “Cornelius!” the angel said.
NLT.4: Cornelius stared at him in terror. “What is it, sir?” he asked the angel. And the angel replied, “Your prayers and gifts to the poor have been received by God as an offering!
NLT.5: Now send some men to Joppa, and summon a man named Simon Peter.
NLT.6: He is staying with Simon, a tanner who lives near the seashore.”
NLT.7: As soon as the angel was gone, Cornelius called two of his household servants and a devout soldier, one of his personal attendants.
NLT.8: He told them what had happened and sent them off to Joppa.
NLT.9: The next day as Cornelius’s messengers were nearing the town, Peter went up on the flat roof to pray. It was about noon,
NLT.10: and he was hungry. But while a meal was being prepared, he fell into a trance.
NLT.11: He saw the sky open, and something like a large sheet was let down by its four corners.
NLT.12: In the sheet were all sorts of animals, reptiles, and birds.
NLT.13: Then a voice said to him, “Get up, Peter; kill and eat them.”
NLT.14: “No, Lord,” Peter declared. “I have never eaten anything that our Jewish laws have declared impure and unclean. ”
NLT.15: But the voice spoke again: “Do not call something unclean if God has made it clean.”
NLT.16: The same vision was repeated three times. Then the sheet was suddenly pulled up to heaven.
NLT.17: Peter was very perplexed. What could the vision mean? Just then the men sent by Cornelius found Simon’s house. Standing outside the gate,
NLT.18: they asked if a man named Simon Peter was staying there.
NLT.19: Meanwhile, as Peter was puzzling over the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men have come looking for you.
NLT.20: Get up, go downstairs, and go with them without hesitation. Don’t worry, for I have sent them.”
NLT.21: So Peter went down and said, “I’m the man you are looking for. Why have you come?”
NLT.22: They said, “We were sent by Cornelius, a Roman officer. He is a devout and God-fearing man, well respected by all the Jews. A holy angel instructed him to summon you to his house so that he can hear your message.”
NLT.23: So Peter invited the men to stay for the night. The next day he went with them, accompanied by some of the brothers from Joppa.
NLT.24: They arrived in Caesarea the following day. Cornelius was waiting for them and had called together his relatives and close friends.
NLT.25: As Peter entered his home, Cornelius fell at his feet and worshiped him.
NLT.26: But Peter pulled him up and said, “Stand up! I’m a human being just like you!”
NLT.27: So they talked together and went inside, where many others were assembled.
NLT.28: Peter told them, “You know it is against our laws for a Jewish man to enter a Gentile home like this or to associate with you. But God has shown me that I should no longer think of anyone as impure or unclean.
NLT.29: So I came without objection as soon as I was sent for. Now tell me why you sent for me.”
NLT.30: Cornelius replied, “Four days ago I was praying in my house about this same time, three o’clock in the afternoon. Suddenly, a man in dazzling clothes was standing in front of me.
NLT.31: He told me, ‘Cornelius, your prayer has been heard, and your gifts to the poor have been noticed by God!
NLT.32: Now send messengers to Joppa, and summon a man named Simon Peter. He is staying in the home of Simon, a tanner who lives near the seashore.’
NLT.33: So I sent for you at once, and it was good of you to come. Now we are all here, waiting before God to hear the message the Lord has given you.”
NLT.34: Then Peter replied, “I see very clearly that God shows no favoritism.
NLT.35: In every nation he accepts those who fear him and do what is right.
NLT.36: This is the message of Good News for the people of Israel — that there is peace with God through Jesus Christ, who is Lord of all.
NLT.37: You know what happened throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee, after John began preaching his message of baptism.
NLT.38: And you know that God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power. Then Jesus went around doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, for God was with him.
NLT.39: “And we apostles are witnesses of all he did throughout Judea and in Jerusalem. They put him to death by hanging him on a cross,
NLT.40: but God raised him to life on the third day. Then God allowed him to appear,
NLT.41: not to the general public, but to us whom God had chosen in advance to be his witnesses. We were those who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.
NLT.42: And he ordered us to preach everywhere and to testify that Jesus is the one appointed by God to be the judge of all — the living and the dead.
NLT.43: He is the one all the prophets testified about, saying that everyone who believes in him will have their sins forgiven through his name.”
NLT.44: Even as Peter was saying these things, the Holy Spirit fell upon all who were listening to the message.
NLT.45: The Jewish believers who came with Peter were amazed that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out on the Gentiles, too.
NLT.46: For they heard them speaking in other tongues and praising God. Then Peter asked,
NLT.47: “Can anyone object to their being baptized, now that they have received the Holy Spirit just as we did?”
NLT.48: So he gave orders for them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Afterward Cornelius asked him to stay with them for several days.

WEB.1: Now there was a certain man in Caesarea, Cornelius by name, a centurion of what was called the Italian Regiment,
WEB.2: a devout man, and one who feared God with all his house, who gave gifts for the needy generously to the people, and always prayed to God.
WEB.3: At about the ninth hour of the day,  he clearly saw in a vision an angel of God coming to him, and saying to him, “Cornelius!”
WEB.4: He, fastening his eyes on him, and being frightened, said, “What is it, Lord?” He said to him, “Your prayers and your gifts to the needy have gone up for a memorial before God.
WEB.5: Now send men to Joppa, and get Simon, who is also called Peter.
WEB.6: He lodges with a tanner named Simon, whose house is by the seaside.
WEB.7: When the angel who spoke to him had departed, Cornelius called two of his household servants and a devout soldier of those who waited on him continually.
WEB.8: Having explained everything to them, he sent them to Joppa.
WEB.9: Now on the next day as they were on their journey, and got close to the city, Peter went up on the housetop to pray at about noon.
WEB.10: He became hungry and desired to eat, but while they were preparing, he fell into a trance.
WEB.11: He saw heaven opened and a certain container descending to him, like a great sheet let down by four corners on the earth,
WEB.12: in which were all kinds of four-footed animals of the earth, wild animals, reptiles, and birds of the sky.
WEB.13: A voice came to him, “Rise, Peter, kill and eat!”
WEB.14: But Peter said, “Not so, Lord; for I have never eaten anything that is common or unclean.”
WEB.15: A voice came to him again the second time, “What God has cleansed, you must not call unclean.”
WEB.16: This was done three times, and immediately the vessel was received up into heaven.
WEB.17: Now while Peter was very perplexed in himself what the vision which he had seen might mean, behold, the men who were sent by Cornelius, having made inquiry for Simon’s house, stood before the gate,
WEB.18: and called and asked whether Simon, who was also called Peter, was lodging there.
WEB.19: While Peter was pondering the vision, the Spirit said to him, “Behold, three  men seek you.
WEB.20: But arise, get down, and go with them, doubting nothing; for I have sent them.”
WEB.21: Peter went down to the men, and said, “Behold, I am he whom you seek. Why have you come?”
WEB.22: They said, “Cornelius, a centurion, a righteous man and one who fears God, and well spoken of by all the nation of the Jews, was directed by a holy angel to invite you to his house, and to listen to what you say.”
WEB.23: So he called them in and provided a place to stay. On the next day Peter arose and went out with them, and some of the brothers from Joppa accompanied him.
WEB.24: On the next day they entered into Caesarea. Cornelius was waiting for them, having called together his relatives and his near friends.
WEB.25: When Peter entered, Cornelius met him, fell down at his feet, and worshiped him.
WEB.26: But Peter raised him up, saying, “Stand up! I myself am also a man.”
WEB.27: As he talked with him, he went in and found many gathered together.
WEB.28: He said to them, “You yourselves know how it is an unlawful thing for a man who is a Jew to join himself or come to one of another nation, but God has shown me that I shouldn’t call any man unholy or unclean.
WEB.29: Therefore also I came without complaint when I was sent for. I ask therefore, why did you send for me?”
WEB.30: Cornelius said, “Four days ago, I was fasting until this hour, and at the ninth hour,  I prayed in my house, and behold, a man stood before me in bright clothing,
WEB.31: and said, ‘Cornelius, your prayer is heard, and your gifts to the needy are remembered in the sight of God.
WEB.32: Send therefore to Joppa, and summon Simon, who is also called Peter. He lodges in the house of a tanner named Simon, by the seaside. When he comes, he will speak to you.’
WEB.33: Therefore I sent to you at once, and it was good of you to come. Now therefore we are all here present in the sight of God to hear all things that have been commanded you by God.”
WEB.34: Peter opened his mouth and said, “Truly I perceive that God doesn’t show favoritism;
WEB.35: but in every nation he who fears him and works righteousness is acceptable to him.
WEB.36: The word which he sent to the children of Israel, preaching good news of peace by Jesus Christ — he is Lord of all —
WEB.37: you yourselves know what happened, which was proclaimed throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee, after the baptism which John preached;
WEB.38: even Jesus of Nazareth, how God anointed him with the Holy Spirit and with power, who went about doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, for God was with him.
WEB.39: We are witnesses of everything he did both in the country of the Jews, and in Jerusalem; whom they also  killed, hanging him on a tree.
WEB.40: God raised him up the third day, and gave him to be revealed,
WEB.41: not to all the people, but to witnesses who were chosen before by God, to us, who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.
WEB.42: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that this is he who is appointed by God as the Judge of the living and the dead.
WEB.43: All the prophets testify about him, that through his name everyone who believes in him will receive remission of sins.”
WEB.44: While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell on all those who heard the word.
WEB.45: They of the circumcision who believed were amazed, as many as came with Peter, because the gift of the Holy Spirit was also poured out on the Gentiles.
WEB.46: For they heard them speaking in other languages and magnifying God. Then Peter answered,
WEB.47: “Can anyone forbid these people from being baptized with water? They have received the Holy Spirit just like us.”
WEB.48: He commanded them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked him to stay some days.

CSB17.1: There was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion of what was called the Italian Regiment. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.2: He was a devout man and feared God along with his whole household. He did many charitable deeds for the Jewish people and always prayed to God. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.3: About three in the afternoon <sup>[1]</sup> he distinctly saw in a vision an angel of God who came in and said to him, “Cornelius.” <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.4: Staring at him in awe, he said, “What is it, Lord?” The angel told him, “Your prayers and your acts of charity have ascended as a memorial offering before God. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.5: Now send men to Joppa and call for Simon, who is also named Peter.
CSB17.6: He is lodging with Simon, a tanner, whose house is by the sea.” <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.7: When the angel who spoke to him had gone, he called two of his household servants and a devout soldier, who was one of those who attended him.
CSB17.8: After explaining everything to them, he sent them to Joppa.
CSB17.9: The <sup>ⓒ</sup> next day, as they were traveling and nearing the city, Peter went up to pray on the roof <sup>ⓓ</sup> about noon. <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.10: He became hungry and wanted to eat, but while they were preparing something, he fell into a trance.
CSB17.11: He saw heaven opened <sup>ⓔ</sup> and an object that resembled a large sheet coming down, being lowered by its four corners to the earth.
CSB17.12: In it were all the four-footed animals and reptiles of the earth, and the birds of the sky.
CSB17.13: A voice said to him, “Get up, Peter; kill and eat.”
CSB17.14: “No, Lord!” Peter said. “For I have never eaten anything impure and ritually unclean.” <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.15: Again, a second time, the voice said to him, “What God has made clean, do not call impure.” <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.16: This happened three times, and suddenly the object was taken up into heaven.
CSB17.17: While Peter was deeply perplexed about what the vision he had seen might mean, right away the men who had been sent by Cornelius, having asked directions to Simon’s house, stood at the gate.
CSB17.18: They called out, asking if Simon, who was also named Peter, was lodging there.
CSB17.19: While Peter was thinking about the vision, the Spirit told him, “Three men are here looking for you. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.20: Get up, go downstairs, and go with them with no doubts at all, because I have sent them.” <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.21: Then Peter went down to the men and said, “Here I am, the one you’re looking for. What is the reason you’re here?”
CSB17.22: They said, “Cornelius, a centurion, an upright and God-fearing man, who has a good reputation with the whole Jewish nation, was divinely directed by a holy angel to call you to his house and to hear a message from you.” <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.23: Peter then invited them in and gave them lodging. The next day he got up and set out with them, and some of the brothers from Joppa went with him. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.24: The following day he entered Caesarea. Now Cornelius was expecting them and had called together his relatives and close friends.
CSB17.25: When Peter entered, Cornelius met him, fell at his feet, and worshiped him.
CSB17.26: But Peter lifted him up and said, “Stand up. I myself am also a man.” <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.27: While talking with him, he went in and found a large gathering of people.
CSB17.28: Peter said to them, “You know it’s forbidden for a Jewish man to associate with or visit a foreigner, <sup>ⓜ</sup> but God has shown me that I must not call any person impure or unclean. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.29: That’s why I came without any objection when I was sent for. So may I ask why you sent for me?”
CSB17.30: Cornelius replied, “Four days ago at this hour, at three in the afternoon, <sup>[3]</sup> I was <sup>[4]</sup> praying in my house. Just then a man in dazzling clothing stood before me <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.31: and said, ‘Cornelius, your prayer has been heard, and your acts of charity have been remembered in God’s sight.
CSB17.32: Therefore send someone to Joppa and invite Simon here, who is also named Peter. He is lodging in Simon the tanner’s house by the sea.’ <sup>[5]</sup>
CSB17.33: So I immediately sent for you, and it was good of you to come. So now we are all in the presence of God to hear everything you have been commanded by the Lord.”
CSB17.34: Peter began to speak: “Now I truly understand that God doesn’t show favoritism, <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.35: but in every nation the person who fears him and does what is right is acceptable to him.
CSB17.36: He sent the message to the Israelites, proclaiming the good news of peace through Jesus Christ — he is Lord of all. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.37: You know the events that took place throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee after the baptism that John preached:
CSB17.38: how God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power, and how he went about doing good and healing all who were under the tyranny of the devil, because God was with him. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.39: We ourselves are witnesses of everything he did in both the Judean country and in Jerusalem, and yet they killed him by hanging him on a tree. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.40: God raised up this man on the third day and caused him to be seen,
CSB17.41: not by all the people, but by us whom God appointed as witnesses, who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.42: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one appointed by God to be the judge of the living and the dead. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.43: All the prophets testify <sup>ⓗ</sup> about him that through his name <sup>ⓘ</sup> everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins.” <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.44: While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit came down <sup>ⓚ</sup> on all those who heard the message.
CSB17.45: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were amazed because the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.46: For they heard them speaking in other tongues <sup>[6]</sup> and declaring the greatness of God. Then Peter responded,
CSB17.47: “Can anyone withhold water and prevent these people from being baptized, who have received the Holy Spirit just as we have?” <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.48: He commanded them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. <sup>ⓝ</sup> Then they asked him to stay for a few days.

AMP.1: Now at Caesarea [Maritima] there was a man named Cornelius, a centurion of what was known as the Italian Regiment,
AMP.2: a devout man and one who, along with all his household, feared God. He made many charitable donations to the Jewish people, and prayed to God always.
AMP.3: About the ninth hour (3:00 p.m.) of the day he clearly saw in a vision an angel of God who had come to him and said, "Cornelius!"
AMP.4: Cornelius was frightened and stared intently at him and said, "What is it, lord (sir)?" And the angel said to him, "Your prayers and gifts of charity have ascended as a memorial offering before God [an offering made in remembrance of His past blessings].
AMP.5: Now send men to Joppa and have them call for a man named Simon, who is also called Peter [and invite him here];
AMP.6: he is staying with Simon the tanner, whose house is by the sea."
AMP.7: When the angel who was speaking to him had gone, Cornelius called two of his servants and a devout soldier from among his own personal attendants;
AMP.8: and after explaining everything to them, he sent them to Joppa.
AMP.9: The next day, as they were on their way and were approaching the city, Peter went up on the roof of the house about the sixth hour (noon) to pray,
AMP.10: but he became hungry and wanted something to eat. While the meal was being prepared he fell into a trance;
AMP.11: and he saw the sky opened up, and an object like a great sheet descending, lowered by its four corners to the earth,
AMP.12: and it contained all kinds of four-footed animals and crawling creatures of the earth and birds of the air.
AMP.13: A voice came to him, "Get up, Peter, kill and eat!"
AMP.14: But Peter said, "Not at all, Lord, for I have never eaten anything that is common (unholy) and [ceremonially] unclean."
AMP.15: And the voice came to him a second time, "What God has cleansed and pronounced clean, no longer consider common (unholy)."
AMP.16: This happened three times, and then immediately the object was taken up into heaven.
AMP.17: Now Peter was still perplexed and completely at a loss as to what his vision could mean when the men who had been sent by Cornelius, having asked directions to Simon's house, arrived at the gate.
AMP.18: And they called out to ask whether Simon, who was also called Peter, was staying there.
AMP.19: While Peter was thoughtfully considering the vision, the Spirit said to him, "Now listen, three men are looking for you.
AMP.20: Get up, go downstairs and go with them without hesitating or doubting, because I have sent them Myself."
AMP.21: Peter went down to the men and said, "I am the one you are looking for. For what reason have you come?"
AMP.22: They said, "Cornelius, a centurion, an upright and God-fearing man well spoken of by all the Jewish people, was divinely instructed by a holy angel to send for you to come to his house and hear what you have to say."
AMP.23:
So Peter invited them in and gave them lodging [for the night].
The next day Peter got up and left with them, and some of the brothers from Joppa went with him.
AMP.24: On the following day he [and the others] entered Caesarea. Cornelius was waiting for them, and had called together his relatives and close friends.
AMP.25: When Peter arrived, Cornelius met him, and fell down at his feet and worshiped him.
AMP.26: But Peter raised him up, saying, "Stand up; I too am only a man."
AMP.27: As Peter talked with him, he entered [the house] and found a large group of people assembled.
AMP.28: He said to them, "You know that it is unlawful for a Jewish man to associate with or befriend a Gentile, or to visit him; and yet God has shown me that I am not to call anyone common or [ceremonially] unclean.
AMP.29: Therefore when I was sent for, I came without raising an objection. So I ask for what reason have you sent for me?"
AMP.30: Cornelius said, "Four days ago to this hour, I was praying in my house during the ninth hour (3:00-4:00 p.m.); and a man [dressed] in bright, dazzling clothing suddenly stood before me,
AMP.31: and he said, 'Cornelius, your prayer has been heard, and your acts of charity have been remembered before God [so that He is about to help you].
AMP.32: Therefore send word to Joppa and invite Simon, who is also called Peter, to come to you. He is staying at the house of Simon the tanner by the sea.'
AMP.33: So I sent for you at once, and you have been kind enough to come. Now then, we are all here present before God to listen to everything that you have been instructed by the Lord [to say]."
AMP.34:
Opening his mouth, Peter said:
"Most certainly I understand now that God is not one to show partiality [to people as though Gentiles were excluded from God's blessing],
AMP.35: but in every nation the person who fears God and does what is right [by seeking Him] is acceptable and welcomed by Him.
AMP.36: You know the message which He sent to the sons of Israel, announcing the good news of peace through Jesus Christ, who is Lord of all—
AMP.37: you know the things that have taken place throughout Judea, starting in Galilee after the baptism preached by John—
AMP.38: how God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with great power; and He went around doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, because God was with Him.
AMP.39: We are [personally] eyewitnesses of everything that He did both in the land of the Jews and in Jerusalem [in particular]. They also put Him to death by hanging Him on a cross;
AMP.40: God raised Him [to life] on the third day and caused Him to be plainly seen,
AMP.41: not to all the people, but to witnesses who were chosen and designated beforehand by God, that is, to us who ate and drank together with Him after He rose from the dead.
AMP.42: He commanded us to preach to the people [both Jew and Gentile], and to solemnly testify that He is the One who has been appointed and ordained by God as Judge of the living and the dead.
AMP.43: All the prophets testify about Him, that through His name everyone who believes in Him [whoever trusts in and relies on Him, accepting Him as Savior and Messiah] receives forgiveness of sins."
AMP.44: While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell on all those who were listening to the message [confirming God's acceptance of Gentiles].
AMP.45: All the circumcised believers who came with Peter were amazed, because the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles.
AMP.46: For they heard them talking in [unknown] tongues (languages) and exalting and magnifying and praising God. Then Peter said,
AMP.47: "Can anyone refuse water for these people to be baptized, since they have received the Holy Spirit just as we did?"
AMP.48: And he ordered that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked him to stay there for a few days.

MSG.1: There was a man named Cornelius who lived in Caesarea, captain of the Italian Guard stationed there.
MSG.2: He was a thoroughly good man. He had led everyone in his house to live worshipfully before God, was always helping people in need, and had the habit of prayer.
MSG.3: One day about three o'clock in the afternoon he had a vision. An angel of God, as real as his next-door neighbor, came in and said, "Cornelius."
MSG.4: Cornelius stared hard, wondering if he was seeing things. Then he said, "What do you want, sir?" The angel said, "Your prayers and neighborly acts have brought you to God's attention.
MSG.5: Here's what you are to do. Send men to Joppa to get Simon, the one everyone calls Peter.
MSG.6: He is staying with Simon the Tanner, whose house is down by the sea."
MSG.7: As soon as the angel was gone, Cornelius called two servants and one particularly devout soldier from the guard.
MSG.8: He went over with them in great detail everything that had just happened, and then sent them off to Joppa.
MSG.9: The next day as the three travelers were approaching the town, Peter went out on the balcony to pray. It was about noon.
MSG.10: Peter got hungry and started thinking about lunch. While lunch was being prepared, he fell into a trance.
MSG.11: He saw the skies open up. Something that looked like a huge blanket lowered by ropes at its four corners settled on the ground.
MSG.12: Every kind of animal and reptile and bird you could think of was on it.
MSG.13: Then a voice came: "Go to it, Peter—kill and eat."
MSG.14: Peter said, "Oh, no, Lord. I've never so much as tasted food that was not kosher."
MSG.15: The voice came a second time: "If God says it's okay, it's okay."
MSG.16: This happened three times, and then the blanket was pulled back up into the skies.
MSG.17: As Peter, puzzled, sat there trying to figure out what it all meant, the men sent by Cornelius showed up at Simon's front door.
MSG.18: They called in, asking if there was a Simon, also called Peter, staying there.
MSG.19: Peter, lost in thought, didn't hear them, so the Spirit whispered to him, "Three men are knocking at the door looking for you.
MSG.20: Get down there and go with them. Don't ask any questions. I sent them to get you."
MSG.21: Peter went down and said to the men, "I think I'm the man you're looking for. What's up?"
MSG.22: They said, "Captain Cornelius, a God-fearing man well-known for his fair play—ask any Jew in this part of the country—was commanded by a holy angel to get you and bring you to his house so he could hear what you had to say."
MSG.23: Peter invited them in and made them feel at home. The next morning he got up and went with them. Some of his friends from Joppa went along.
MSG.24: A day later they entered Caesarea. Cornelius was expecting them and had his relatives and close friends waiting with him.
MSG.25: The minute Peter came through the door, Cornelius was up on his feet greeting him—and then down on his face worshiping him!
MSG.26: Peter pulled him up and said, "None of that—I'm a man and only a man, no different from you."
MSG.27: Talking things over, they went on into the house, where Cornelius introduced Peter to everyone who had come.
MSG.28: Peter addressed them, "You know, I'm sure that this is highly irregular. Jews just don't do this—visit and relax with people of another race. But God has just shown me that no race is better than any other.
MSG.29: So the minute I was sent for, I came, no questions asked. But now I'd like to know why you sent for me."
MSG.30: Cornelius said, "Four days ago at about this time, midafternoon, I was home praying. Suddenly there was a man right in front of me, flooding the room with light.
MSG.31: He said, 'Cornelius, your daily prayers and neighborly acts have brought you to God's attention.
MSG.32: I want you to send to Joppa to get Simon, the one they call Peter. He's staying with Simon the Tanner down by the sea.'
MSG.33: "So I did it—I sent for you. And you've been good enough to come. And now we're all here in God's presence, ready to listen to whatever the Master put in your heart to tell us."
MSG.34: Peter fairly exploded with his good news: "It's God's own truth, nothing could be plainer: God plays no favorites!
MSG.35: It makes no difference who you are or where you're from—if you want God and are ready to do as he says, the door is open.
MSG.36: The Message he sent to the children of Israel—that through Jesus Christ everything is being put together again—well, he's doing it everywhere, among everyone.
MSG.37: "You know the story of what happened in Judea. It began in Galilee after John preached a total life-change.
MSG.38: Then Jesus arrived from Nazareth, anointed by God with the Holy Spirit, ready for action. He went through the country helping people and healing everyone who was beaten down by the Devil. He was able to do all this because God was with him.
MSG.39: "And we saw it, saw it all, everything he did in the land of the Jews and in Jerusalem where they killed him, hung him from a cross.
MSG.40: But in three days God had him up, alive, and out where he could be seen.
MSG.41: Not everyone saw him—he wasn't put on public display. Witnesses had been carefully handpicked by God beforehand—us! We were the ones, there to eat and drink with him after he came back from the dead.
MSG.42: He commissioned us to announce this in public, to bear solemn witness that he is in fact the One whom God destined as Judge of the living and dead.
MSG.43: But we're not alone in this. Our witness that he is the means to forgiveness of sins is backed up by the witness of all the prophets."
MSG.44: No sooner were these words out of Peter's mouth than the Holy Spirit came on the listeners.
MSG.45: The believing Jews who had come with Peter couldn't believe it, couldn't believe that the gift of the Holy Spirit was poured out on "outsider" Gentiles,
MSG.46: but there it was—they heard them speaking in tongues, heard them praising God. Then Peter said,
MSG.47: "Do I hear any objections to baptizing these friends with water? They've received the Holy Spirit exactly as we did."
MSG.48: Hearing no objections, he ordered that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked Peter to stay on for a few days.

NIV.1:
Cornelius Calls for Peter
At Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, a centurion in what was known as the Italian Regiment.
NIV.2: He and all his family were devout and God-fearing; he gave generously to those in need and prayed to God regularly.
NIV.3: One day at about three in the afternoon he had a vision. He distinctly saw an angel of God, who came to him and said, “Cornelius!”
NIV.4:
Cornelius stared at him in fear. “What is it, Lord?” he asked.
The angel answered, “Your prayers and gifts to the poor have come up as a memorial offering before God.
NIV.5: Now send men to Joppa to bring back a man named Simon who is called Peter.
NIV.6: He is staying with Simon the tanner, whose house is by the sea.”
NIV.7: When the angel who spoke to him had gone, Cornelius called two of his servants and a devout soldier who was one of his attendants.
NIV.8: He told them everything that had happened and sent them to Joppa.
NIV.9:
Peter’s Vision
About noon the following day as they were on their journey and approaching the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray.
NIV.10: He became hungry and wanted something to eat, and while the meal was being prepared, he fell into a trance.
NIV.11: He saw heaven opened and something like a large sheet being let down to earth by its four corners.
NIV.12: It contained all kinds of four-footed animals, as well as reptiles of the earth and birds of the air.
NIV.13: Then a voice told him, “Get up, Peter. Kill and eat.”
NIV.14: “Surely not, Lord!” Peter replied. “I have never eaten anything impure or unclean.”
NIV.15: The voice spoke to him a second time, “Do not call anything impure that God has made clean.”
NIV.16: This happened three times, and immediately the sheet was taken back to heaven.
NIV.17: While Peter was wondering about the meaning of the vision, the men sent by Cornelius found out where Simon’s house was and stopped at the gate.
NIV.18: They called out, asking if Simon who was known as Peter was staying there.
NIV.19: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Spirit said to him, “Simon, three men are looking for you.
NIV.20: So get up and go downstairs. Do not hesitate to go with them, for I have sent them.”
NIV.21: Peter went down and said to the men, “I’m the one you’re looking for. Why have you come?”
NIV.22: The men replied, “We have come from Cornelius the centurion. He is a righteous and God-fearing man, who is respected by all the Jewish people. A holy angel told him to have you come to his house so that he could hear what you have to say.”
NIV.23:
Then Peter invited the men into the house to be his guests.
Peter at Cornelius’s House
The next day Peter started out with them, and some of the brothers from Joppa went along.
NIV.24: The following day he arrived in Caesarea. Cornelius was expecting them and had called together his relatives and close friends.
NIV.25: As Peter entered the house, Cornelius met him and fell at his feet in reverence.
NIV.26: But Peter made him get up. “Stand up,” he said, “I am only a man myself.”
NIV.27: Talking with him, Peter went inside and found a large gathering of people.
NIV.28: He said to them: “You are well aware that it is against our law for a Jew to associate with a Gentile or visit him. But God has shown me that I should not call any man impure or unclean.
NIV.29: So when I was sent for, I came without raising any objection. May I ask why you sent for me?”
NIV.30: Cornelius answered: “Four days ago I was in my house praying at this hour, at three in the afternoon. Suddenly a man in shining clothes stood before me
NIV.31: and said, ‘Cornelius, God has heard your prayer and remembered your gifts to the poor.
NIV.32: Send to Joppa for Simon who is called Peter. He is a guest in the home of Simon the tanner, who lives by the sea.’
NIV.33: So I sent for you immediately, and it was good of you to come. Now we are all here in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has commanded you to tell us.”
NIV.34: Then Peter began to speak: “I now realize how true it is that God does not show favoritism
NIV.35: but accepts men from every nation who fear him and do what is right.
NIV.36: You know the message God sent to the people of Israel, telling the good news of peace through Jesus Christ, who is Lord of all.
NIV.37: You know what has happened throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism that John preached—
NIV.38: how God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and power, and how he went around doing good and healing all who were under the power of the devil, because God was with him.
NIV.39: “We are witnesses of everything he did in the country of the Jews and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree,
NIV.40: but God raised him from the dead on the third day and caused him to be seen.
NIV.41: He was not seen by all the people, but by witnesses whom God had already chosen—by us who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.
NIV.42: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one whom God appointed as judge of the living and the dead.
NIV.43: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.”
NIV.44: While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit came on all who heard the message.
NIV.45: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles.
NIV.46:
For they heard them speaking in tongues and praising God.
Then Peter said,
NIV.47: “Can anyone keep these people from being baptized with water? They have received the Holy Spirit just as we have.”
NIV.48: So he ordered that they be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked Peter to stay with them for a few days.

NRSVCE.37: That message spread throughout Judea, beginning in Galilee after the baptism that John announced:
NRSVCE.38: how God anointed Jesus of Nazareth with the Holy Spirit and with power; how he went about doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, for God was with him.
NRSVCE.39: We are witnesses to all that he did both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They put him to death by hanging him on a tree;
NRSVCE.40: but God raised him on the third day and allowed him to appear,
NRSVCE.41: not to all the people but to us who were chosen by God as witnesses, and who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.
NRSVCE.42: He commanded us to preach to the people and to testify that he is the one ordained by God as judge of the living and the dead.
NRSVCE.43: All the prophets testify about him that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.”
NRSVCE.44: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit fell upon all who heard the word.
NRSVCE.45: The circumcised believers who had come with Peter were astounded that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles,
NRSVCE.46: for they heard them speaking in tongues and extolling God. Then Peter said,
NRSVCE.47: “Can anyone withhold the water for baptizing these people who have received the Holy Spirit just as we have?”
NRSVCE.48: So he ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they invited him to stay for several days.

NET.1: Now there was a man in Caesarea named Cornelius, a centurion of what was known as the Italian Cohort.
NET.2: He was a devout, God-fearing man, as was all his household; he did many acts of charity for the people and prayed to God regularly.
NET.3: About three o’clock one afternoon he saw clearly in a vision an angel of God who came in and said to him, “Cornelius.”
NET.4: Staring at him and becoming greatly afraid, Cornelius replied, “What is it, Lord?” The angel said to him, “Your prayers and your acts of charity have gone up as a memorial before God.
NET.5: Now send men to Joppa and summon a man named Simon, who is called Peter.
NET.6: This man is staying as a guest with a man named Simon, a tanner, whose house is by the sea.”
NET.7: When the angel who had spoken to him departed, Cornelius called two of his personal servants and a devout soldier from among those who served him,
NET.8: and when he had explained everything to them, he sent them to Joppa.
NET.9: About noon the next day, while they were on their way and approaching the city, Peter went up on the roof to pray.
NET.10: He became hungry and wanted to eat, but while they were preparing the meal, a trance came over him.
NET.11: He saw heaven opened and an object something like a large sheet descending, being let down to earth by its four corners.
NET.12: In it were all kinds of four-footed animals and reptiles of the earth and wild birds.
NET.13: Then a voice said to him, “Get up, Peter; slaughter and eat!”
NET.14: But Peter said, “Certainly not, Lord, for I have never eaten anything defiled and ritually unclean!”
NET.15: The voice spoke to him again, a second time, “What God has made clean, you must not consider ritually unclean!”
NET.16: This happened three times, and immediately the object was taken up into heaven.
NET.17: Now while Peter was puzzling over what the vision he had seen could signify, the men sent by Cornelius had learned where Simon’s house was and approached the gate.
NET.18: They called out to ask if Simon, known as Peter, was staying there as a guest.
NET.19: While Peter was still thinking seriously about the vision, the Spirit said to him, “Look! Three men are looking for you.
NET.20: But get up, go down, and accompany them without hesitation, because I have sent them.”
NET.21: So Peter went down to the men and said, “Here I am, the person you’re looking for. Why have you come?”
NET.22: They said, “Cornelius the centurion, a righteous and God-fearing man, well spoken of by the whole Jewish nation, was directed by a holy angel to summon you to his house and to hear a message from you.”
NET.23: So Peter invited them in and entertained them as guests. On the next day he got up and set out with them, and some of the brothers from Joppa accompanied him.
NET.24: The following day he entered Caesarea. Now Cornelius was waiting anxiously for them and had called together his relatives and close friends.
NET.25: So when Peter came in, Cornelius met him, fell at his feet, and worshiped him.
NET.26: But Peter helped him up, saying, “Stand up. I too am a mere mortal.”
NET.27: Peter continued talking with him as he went in, and he found many people gathered together.
NET.28: He said to them, “You know that it is unlawful for a Jew to associate with or visit a Gentile, yet God has shown me that I should call no person defiled or ritually unclean.
NET.29: Therefore when you sent for me, I came without any objection. Now may I ask why you sent for me?”
NET.30: Cornelius replied, “Four days ago at this very hour, at three o’clock in the afternoon, I was praying in my house, and suddenly a man in shining clothing stood before me
NET.31: and said, ‘Cornelius, your prayer has been heard and your acts of charity have been remembered before God.
NET.32: Therefore send to Joppa and summon Simon, who is called Peter. This man is staying as a guest in the house of Simon the tanner, by the sea.’
NET.33: Therefore I sent for you at once, and you were kind enough to come. So now we are all here in the presence of God to listen to everything the Lord has commanded you to say to us.”
NET.34: Then Peter started speaking: “I now truly understand that God does not show favoritism in dealing with people,
NET.35: but in every nation the person who fears him and does what is right is welcomed before him.
NET.36: You know the message he sent to the people of Israel, proclaiming the good news of peace through Jesus Christ (he is Lord of all) —
NET.37: you know what happened throughout Judea, beginning from Galilee after the baptism that John announced:
NET.38: with respect to Jesus from Nazareth, that God anointed him with the Holy Spirit and with power. He went around doing good and healing all who were oppressed by the devil, because God was with him.
NET.39: We are witnesses of all the things he did both in Judea and in Jerusalem. They killed him by hanging him on a tree,
NET.40: but God raised him up on the third day and caused him to be seen,
NET.41: not by all the people, but by us, the witnesses God had already chosen, who ate and drank with him after he rose from the dead.
NET.42: He commanded us to preach to the people and to warn them that he is the one appointed by God as judge of the living and the dead.
NET.43: About him all the prophets testify, that everyone who believes in him receives forgiveness of sins through his name.”
NET.44: While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell on all those who heard the message.
NET.45: The circumcised believers who had accompanied Peter were greatly astonished that the gift of the Holy Spirit had been poured out even on the Gentiles,
NET.46: for they heard them speaking in tongues and praising God. Then Peter said,
NET.47: “No one can withhold the water for these people to be baptized, who have received the Holy Spirit just as we did, can he?”
NET.48: So he gave orders to have them baptized in the name of Jesus Christ. Then they asked him to stay for several days.
